description: Result category copy for both quiz paths. Consumed by the result screen renderer.

business.description: AI Readiness Score — 0 to 80 effective. Tier ranges adjusted for v2 scoring. Each tier maps to a named category with a tagline, description, and 3 insight cards.

categories.label: AI-Ready Operator
categories.tagline: You're not waiting for AI. You're running it.
categories.description: You're not just AI-aware — you're positioned to implement and scale. You have the systems, the mindset, and the momentum to move fast. The gap between where you are and a fully automated operation is a matter of weeks, not years.

insight_cards.title: Deploy a 24/7 AI front desk
insight_cards.body: A trained AI voice + chat receptionist handles inbound calls, books appointments, answers FAQs, and qualifies leads around the clock — without adding a single staff hour. Most operators see a measurable lift in booked appointments within the first 30 days.

insight_cards.title: Automate your entire lead follow-up
insight_cards.body: Every cold lead, no-show, and ghosted inquiry is a revenue leak. Pair AI with your CRM to trigger personalized follow-up sequences automatically — the right message, the right timing, zero manual effort.

insight_cards.title: Get an operations intelligence dashboard
insight_cards.body: Stop running your business on gut feel and end-of-month spreadsheets. An AI-generated weekly report surfaces your top revenue drivers, customer patterns, and staff performance without any data entry.

categories.label: AI-Curious Builder
categories.tagline: You see the value. You just need the right on-ramp.
categories.description: You get it — you're not afraid of AI, and you can see what it would do for your bottom line. You're looking for something that works reliably without a six-month implementation headache. That entry point exists, and it starts with one problem worth solving.

insight_cards.title: Run a 30-day AI pilot on your biggest bottleneck
insight_cards.body: Don't try to automate everything at once. Pick your single most expensive bottleneck — usually appointment booking or lead follow-up — and prove the ROI on that one thing first. A focused 30-day pilot gives you the numbers to justify scaling everything else.

insight_cards.title: Produce 10x more content without hiring anyone
insight_cards.body: AI can generate on-brand social posts, email campaigns, and client communications at a pace no staff hire can match. Set it up once with your voice and style guidelines, and your content calendar fills itself.

insight_cards.title: Eliminate scheduling back-and-forth entirely
insight_cards.body: Every minute spent coordinating appointments is a minute not spent on revenue-generating work. Smart scheduling automation syncs to your calendar, sends confirmations and reminders, handles rescheduling, and reduces no-shows by 20-35%.

categories.label: AI-Aware Explorer
categories.tagline: You know it matters. The path just isn't clear yet.
categories.description: You know AI is changing the game — you're still mapping which moves actually apply to your business. Most businesses at your stage are two or three specific automations away from a meaningful time and revenue unlock. The key is knowing which ones to start with.

insight_cards.title: Get a free AI strategy call first
insight_cards.body: Before buying any tool or building anything, a 45-minute strategy call that maps AI specifically to your business type, team size, and biggest pain points is worth more than months of trial-and-error.

insight_cards.title: Start with a $50/month starter stack
insight_cards.body: You don't need enterprise software to get real value from AI. Claude, ChatGPT, Notion AI, and a Zapier automation can eliminate 20-30% of your repetitive weekly tasks for under $50 a month. Start there, then scale up.

insight_cards.title: Automate your top 10 customer FAQs
insight_cards.body: An AI chat widget trained on your most common questions can free up hours of staff time every week — and make your business look more professional and responsive to every new prospect who visits your site.

categories.label: AI Newcomer
categories.tagline: You're starting at exactly the right time.
categories.description: You're at the beginning of the AI journey — which is actually a strategic advantage right now. You skip the trial-and-error that burned a lot of early adopters and go straight to what's proven to work. No bad implementations to undo, just a clean slate.

insight_cards.title: Get a plain-English AI breakdown for your industry
insight_cards.body: Skip the generic hype. There are 3-5 AI tools already working in businesses exactly like yours — tested, proven, and priced for small business budgets. The right starting point is the one that solves the problem costing you the most time right now.

insight_cards.title: Book a free 30-minute AI audit
insight_cards.body: A no-pressure conversation that maps exactly where AI could save you the most time and money before you spend a single dollar. You'll leave with a ranked list of specific automations tailored to your business — not a sales pitch, an actual roadmap.

insight_cards.title: Try one small thing this week
insight_cards.body: Claude and ChatGPT are both free. Use either to draft one client email or one social post this week. That single 10-minute experience reframes how you see the entire opportunity.

general.description: AI Personality Type — scored 0 to 80 effective. Tier ranges adjusted for v2 scoring (4 scored questions). Each type maps to a named category with identity-affirming copy.

categories.label: The Trailblazer
categories.tagline: AI is already your co-pilot. You're not waiting for the future.
categories.description: You don't just know what AI is — you're actively using it, experimenting with it, and pushing past what most people think it can do. You're ahead of the curve, and the curve is catching up fast.

insight_cards.title: Build your own personal AI workflows
insight_cards.body: You've outgrown using AI tools one at a time. Start chaining them — ChatGPT for drafting, Zapier for routing, Notion AI for organizing — and automate multi-step tasks that most people still do manually.

insight_cards.title: Create an AI that actually knows you
insight_cards.body: Custom GPTs and Claude Projects let you build a personal AI assistant that knows your context, communication style, and preferences. Stop re-explaining yourself to every new conversation.

insight_cards.title: Teach one person something this month
insight_cards.body: You're in the 5% who genuinely understand how to get value from AI. Teaching a friend or family member one concrete use case accelerates your own learning faster than any tutorial.

categories.label: The Explorer
categories.tagline: You're curious, you're experimenting, and you're closer to fluent than you think.
categories.description: You're not watching from the sidelines. You've tried things, you have opinions, and you're building real comfort. The gap between where you are and genuinely fluent isn't skill — it's habit. You're one good workflow away from wondering how you ever managed without it.

insight_cards.title: Solve one weekly task completely with AI
insight_cards.body: Stop spreading your attention across ten tools. Pick the single thing that costs you the most time every week — drafting emails, researching, summarizing content — and go all-in on automating just that.

insight_cards.title: Build a personal prompt library
insight_cards.body: Start saving the prompts that actually work for you. A personal library of 15-20 reliable prompts makes every AI tool three times more useful overnight.

insight_cards.title: Try AI by voice for three days
insight_cards.body: If you haven't explored AI voice tools — Claude on mobile, ChatGPT voice mode — spend three days using voice instead of typing. It unlocks a completely different, faster way of working.

categories.label: The Thoughtful Skeptic
categories.tagline: You're aware, you're cautious, and honestly? That's not a bad place to start.
categories.description: You haven't bought into the hype, and that's the healthy response to a lot of what's out there. But there's a difference between healthy skepticism and staying on the outside of something that's genuinely changing everything. You just need one use case that saves you time.

insight_cards.title: Start with something genuinely tedious
insight_cards.body: AI handles genuinely boring tasks like summarizing long articles, writing the first draft of an awkward email, or organizing a messy to-do list. Start there, with something that already annoys you.

insight_cards.title: Use it without creating an account
insight_cards.body: If privacy is the hesitation, both Claude and ChatGPT let you have a full conversation without creating an account or handing over personal data. Give it one real task anonymously.

insight_cards.title: Give it one actual task this week
insight_cards.body: Not a test. An actual task you were already going to do anyway — draft a message, plan a trip, summarize something. Let AI help. Then form your opinion based on real experience.

categories.label: The Fresh Start
categories.tagline: You're at the beginning — which means you skip all the bad habits everyone else is unlearning.
categories.description: You're not behind. The people who started with AI two years ago spent most of that time doing it wrong. You get to start right now — when the tools are actually good, the use cases are proven, and there are real guides instead of just buzzwords.

insight_cards.title: Have one real conversation first
insight_cards.body: Go to claude.ai or chat.openai.com right now. Type one thing you're curious about. That's the whole first step. The first conversation is the hardest — everything after gets easier fast.

insight_cards.title: Forget the robots. Think about your time.
insight_cards.body: AI isn't about futuristic technology — it's a faster way to draft, research, plan, and brainstorm things you already need to do. Think of it as a capable assistant who never sleeps and never judges a dumb question.

insight_cards.title: Try three specific things before you decide
insight_cards.body: Summarize one long article. Draft one email. Plan one meal or trip. Three concrete, low-stakes tasks this week — then decide what you think based on what actually happened.
